text: fat is 1.3g. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for a legume, stable carbs plus protein is the dream.
carbs is 60g. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.
protein is 23.5g. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.
vitamin e at 1% DV, vitamin c at 3% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
magnesium at 46% DV, iron at 24% DV, potassium at 33% DV stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

the upsides first. filling for the calories because legumes usually earn a lot of practical value from satiety. cheap staple with real payoff because this matters because a food is stronger when people can actually use it often. fibre-forward category identity because legumes are supposed to bring stable carbs and fibre.

on the weaker side. can be rough on digestion for some people because practical tolerance still matters. can take planning or prep because that is a small but real friction cost. protein quality still has a ceiling because good, but not meat or dairy level.
B tier. Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
